title: Capability Gap Assessment 2

source.file: input_data/corpus/20200803 PC-Strategy-Solution Development and GoToMarket PlanVer1.5.xlsx

grain: one row per solution component within a solution area; solution-level fields (#, Area, Solutions, Challenges, Benchmarks, Business Outcomes) populated only on each block's lead row
summary: A capability-gap assessment that breaks each solution (within an Area such as Claim) into solution components and features, rates EXL's capability (Low/Medium/High) for each, describes the capability gap, and flags whether a partnership is required split across three partner types (InsurTech / TPA / Other). It also carries the challenges being solved, benchmarks, and business outcomes per solution. Use it to see where EXL is strong or has gaps by solution component and where partnerships are needed.
use_cases: For each solution component, what is EXL's capability level (Low/Medium/High)?
Which solution components require a partnership, and of what type (InsurTech/TPA/Other)?
What capability gap is described for a given solution area (e.g., Claim / FNOL)?
What benchmarks and business outcomes are tied to each solution?

columns.note: solution block sequence number; lead row only

columns.name: Area
columns.note: solution area, e.g. Claim; lead row only

columns.name: Solutions
columns.note: solution name, e.g. FNOL-as-a-Service; lead row only

columns.name: Solution Components
columns.note: row-level component name, e.g. Omni Channel Intake

columns.name: Challenges we are trying to solve
Partnership Required
columns.note: Y/N — InsurTech partner (row-2 sub-header 'InsurTech' of 'Partnership Required')

examples.Solution Features: Provides a channel agnostic claim reporting solution for customers (broker and insured)
examples.Challenges we are trying to solve: -Brokers and Insureds want the choice of reporting channels (Mobile, Web, Email, Fax, Phone)...[truncated]
examples.Capability Gap Description: Capability exists. 1. Full scale multi-channel implementation has not been done with any client to date 2. Mobile interface and reporting does not exist today
examples.Benchmarks: TAT Non Voice - 95% assigned within 2 hours (most stringent)...[truncated]
examples.Business Outcomes: -Reduce claim costs - ALAE and ULAE; -Streamline and simplify the process...[truncated]

examples.Solution Features: Provides immediate notice to insured and broker that the claim has been received and a claim number assigned

not_answerable.q: How do named competitors (Genpact/WNS/TCS/Cognizant) rate on each capability?
not_answerable.reason: the 'Competition Capability' columns are entirely empty

not_answerable.q: What is the numeric capability score for each component?
not_answerable.reason: this sheet has only a Low/Medium/High rating, no numeric scale column

not_answerable.q: How has the capability gap changed over time?

warnings: two-row header band: row-2 sub-headers InsurTech/TPA/Other (under 'Partnership Required') and Genpact/WNS/TCS/Cognizant (under 'Competition Capability'); profiler-detected header_row corrected to 1
'Partnership Required'/'Unnamed: 9'/'Unnamed: 10' are the InsurTech/TPA/Other Y-N flags
'Competition Capability' and its Unnamed sub-columns are entirely empty
solution-level fields (#/Area/Solutions/Challenges/Benchmarks/Business Outcomes) populated only on block lead rows
near-duplicate column set with: ['Capability Gap Assessment '] (per profiler; that sheet is outside this ingest scope)
